query: SpaceX Starship Flight 12 Version 3 launch date outcome splashdown
a_issues: Content A gives only a very truncated SpaceX snippet that omits the launch date and upper-stage splashdown outcome. Its other evidence is weaker and somewhat inconsistent: the Fandom page is user-edited and includes stale NET dates and planning info rather than the final outcome; the Space.com snippet mixes earlier schedule changes (May 19/20/22) and mainly describes planned splashdown rather than clearly confirming the final result. Overall A requires inference and relies more on less-authoritative sources.
b_issues: Content B includes one weaker source (YouTube) and one X post, but they are consistent with the higher-quality sources. The SpaceX snippet in B is also truncated before explicitly mentioning the ship's Indian Ocean splashdown, though CNN and the YouTube/Space.com results supply that fact. No major factual contradiction is present.
reasoning: B better supports a correct answer because it directly contains the key facts from reliable sources: launch date Friday, May 22, 2026 (Space.com), that this was the first Starship V3/Version 3 flight (SpaceX/Space.com/CNN), and that Starship completed a controlled splashdown in the Indian Ocean while the booster had a hard splashdown in the Gulf (CNN and SpaceX). A has some of these facts, but less directly and with more dependence on a Fandom page and snippets about planned scheduling rather than confirmed outcome. Therefore B more cleanly and accurately enables the LLM to answer the question.
